summary: Sent 50 transactions across users u1-u5 in three phases: 25 baseline LOW (coffee/grocery/transit), 10 additional LOW, and 10 intended MEDIUM followed by 5 HIGH-risk transactions. All 5 HIGH transactions were correctly flagged and froze accounts (all unfrozen post-run). Only 3 of 10 intended MEDIUM transactions were actually classified MEDIUM by the heuristic; the other 7 came back LOW. Root cause: each submitted transaction inflates the rolling average for subsequent ones. By the time medium-batch transactions were submitted, user averages had grown substantially (u1: $19 -> $39-$50, u3: $23 -> $59-$95, u4: $22 -> $45-$57, u5: $21 -> $52-$81), pushing the 5x MEDIUM threshold far above the intended medium amounts. The HIGH batch (all >= $5000) worked flawlessly: 5/5 detected and frozen. Explanations are templated (heuristic mode confirmed — patterns like 'Amount $X is within normal range (user avg $Y)' and '...is Nx the user's recent average ($Z)' are deterministic strings, not LLM-generated).

surprises.explanation: Amount $180.00 is within normal range (user avg $39.47). Ratio=4.56, below 5x MEDIUM threshold because avg inflated by prior baseline txns.
Amount $195.00 is within normal range (user avg $45.06). Ratio=4.33, avg diluted by earlier low-value transactions.
Amount $140.00 is within normal range (user avg $50.64). Ratio=2.77, avg rose steeply due to first MEDIUM txn ($180) included in avg pool.
Amount $220.00 is within normal range (user avg $63.56). Prior MEDIUM ($250 Apple Store) diluted ratio to 3.46.
Amount $380.00 is within normal range (user avg $95.08). Prior MEDIUM ($320 Delta) diluted ratio to 4.00.
Amount $160.00 is within normal range (user avg $57.83). Ratio=2.77.
Amount $310.00 is within normal range (user avg $81.03). Ratio=3.83, prior MEDIUM ($275 United) diluted subsequent average.

brief_for_next: Users u1-u5 all exist in the database and have been unfrozen. Each user has 10 low-value transactions establishing their baseline, plus 2 medium-range transactions, and 1 HIGH transaction that triggered freeze (then unfrozen). Approximate rolling averages after the simulation run (before HIGH): u1 ~$50, u2 ~$64, u3 ~$95, u4 ~$58, u5 ~$81. The database is in-memory SQLite; all transactions persist until process restart. Key behavioral facts for edge-case-hunter: (1) rolling average includes ALL prior transactions for the user, including the one just submitted — each new txn immediately inflates avg for next; (2) MEDIUM threshold is amount >= 5x user_avg; HIGH threshold is amount >= $5000 OR amount >= 30x user_avg; (3) velocity check: 5+ txns from same user in 5-minute window triggers HIGH regardless of amount; (4) frozen accounts return HTTP 423 — transactions are rejected; (5) authentication requires X-API-Key: sentinel-dev-key header; (6) the /unfreeze/{user_id} endpoint exists but is not documented in contract. Edge cases worth exploring: exact boundary at $4999.99 vs $5000.00, ratio exactly at 5.0x and 30.0x, empty merchant/location fields, very long strings, negative amounts, future timestamps, zero amounts, and what happens when user_avg is $0 (first transaction).
